name: Saltopus
tagline: This speedy little hunter was only as big as a domestic cat!
intro: Dinosaurs came in all shapes and sizes. Saltopus was a slim, agile dinosaur that was only as big as a cat! And like a cat, this little dinosaur preyed upon creatures smaller than itself, such as prehistoric lizards and insects. The fossils of Saltopus have been found only in Scotland.

topics.title: Tiny Dinosaurs
topics.text: When most people think of dinosaurs, they imagine giant, slow-moving reptiles. But dinosaurs came in many sizes, and some, like these tiny ones, were lightning fast.

captions.label: Pet-sized dinosaur
captions.text: Saltopus was so small that if it were alive today, some people might even keep one as a pet!

captions.label: Lesothosaurus
captions.text: This little dinosaur measured only three feet in length. Its flat, leaf-shaped teeth and beak suggest it probably fed on plants.

captions.label: Compsognathus
captions.text: This dinosaur's name means "pretty jaw." But its toothy grin wouldn't have looked so pretty to the small animals it hunted.

_provenance.note: Text liberated from original screen art; images & audio restored from disc. Original media is Microsoft/supplier copyright — non-commercial educational preservation.
